blurb: Circa Survive has a way of building songs that feel like they're falling apart and holding together at exactly the same time, and "Act Appalled" is a masterclass in that tension. The guitars spiral around Anthony Green's vocals like they're having an argument, each one convinced it's right, and by the time the song hits its peak it's genuinely unclear which one wins. This one's cemented by a Riot Fest memory, the kind of moment where the crowd suddenly goes quiet right before everything breaks open—which, if you know Circa, is basically their whole deal.

Loathe's "Gifted Every Strength" builds like a system stress-testing itself—all coiled tension and precise mechanics until it just *breaks open*, and there's this moment around the three-minute mark where the song stops pretending to be controlled and becomes something genuinely overwhelming. It's the kind of track that rewards patience before it rewards catharsis, which probably explains why it's become the current favorite: it doesn't announce itself, it earns its space in your head.

The solo on this one is genuinely worth learning if you've got a guitar—it's the kind of thing that sounds impossible until you slow it down and realize it's just *relentless* precision disguised as chaos. Claudio Sanchez built something here that's technically demanding but still melodic enough to feel like a real song rather than a technical exercise, which is why people actually want to learn it instead of just respecting it from a distance.
